phrasal_verbs.meaning: find or see someone or something when you did not expect to
meet someone or find something by chance
become cooler, or make something cooler
reduce the amount of something, especially money that you spend
become weaker or less common and then disappear completely
remove something from under the ground by digging; dig holes in an area of land; find information by searching carefully
if something dries up or is dried up, all the water comes out of it; stop being available
use or finish something; manage to deal with a difficult situation or stay alive until it is over; finish dealing with some work, a subject, etc
destroy living things so that most or all of them are dead
if you put something down to a particular reason, you think it has happened for that reason
appear or be produced suddenly and quickly
keep a lot of something so that you can use it later; do something that will cause problems in the future

get rid of something that you no longer want, for example by putting it in a dustbin; force someone to leave a place or group; if someone in authority throws out a plan, proposal, etc they refuse to accept it
produce something new or unexpected; cause something such as dust or water to rise into the air

odds: the odds are in favour of/against

idioms.meaning: an event such as a flood, lightning or an earthquake that is produced by natural forces, and that people have no control over
used for saying that something always happens or someone always does something despite bad weather or difficult conditions
having a long period of difficulty in doing work and earning money
be chosen to do something unpleasant
allow something to develop without trying to influence it
used for emphasising that there is no reason or explanation for something happening in a way that is sudden and unexpected, and does not seem connected with anything that happened before
used when you have said that you have had good luck in order to prevent bad luck from happening to you
used for saying people have limited control over what life brings them
used when you have said that you have had good luck in order to prevent bad luck from happening to you

A.title: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box
A.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

questions.sentence: The elderly fortune teller claimed she could ……………………. upcoming events, yet her predictions rarely matched reality.
Feeling reckless one evening, he chose to ……………………… his entire savings on a single hand of cards.
Researchers discovered that one of the bacterial genes had ………………………… , producing a strain resistant to antibiotics.
The unusual weather pattern was ……………………… to a dramatic shift in ocean currents.
The sudden clap of thunder completely ……………………… the cat sleeping on the windowsill.
Share prices tend to ……………………… wildly during periods of political uncertainty.
Investigators are still working to ……………………… what triggered the sudden collapse of the bridge.
The toddler took her first steps and began to ……………………………… unsteadily before tumbling onto the cushions.
No system can reliably help you ……………………… the winning lottery numbers, whatever anyone claims.
In its final stretch, the old river begins to ……………………… lazily through the green valley below.

B.title: Choose the correct answer
B.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C or D) to complete each sentence.

questions.sentence: Do you know the ………………… time it takes to drive from here to the coast?
She wore a small golden ………………… around her wrist that she said would bring her good fortune.
Running into your old teacher at a foreign airport – what an extraordinary ………………… !
The coach insisted that the foul was not ………………… , but the referee remained unconvinced.
Historians can only ………………… about what really caused the fall of that ancient empire.
The ………………… of winning a professional tennis tournament at the age of sixty is essentially zero.
I hadn't studied the economics chapter at all, so getting that question right was sheer ………………… .
Are you really going to avoid room thirteen because of some old ………………… ?

C.title: Complete using the correct form of the words in the boxes
C.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word from the box.

questions.sentence: It was completely …………………  – she just grabbed her coat and suggested we drive to the mountains for the weekend.
The storage room had been organised in such a ………………… way that nothing could ever be found easily.
Failing the driving test for the third time was a real ………………… to his confidence.
The school trip went smoothly overall, despite a few minor ………………… on the first day.

D.title: Correct the wrong words in bold
D.instructions: If the word or phrase in bold is correct, put OK. If it is incorrect, replace it with a word or phrase in bold from another sentence.

questions.sentence: The coastal flooding was a terrible **resource** that left thousands of families without homes.
Authorities announced plans to **harvest** all residents from the low-lying areas before the storm arrived.
Experts warn that our over-reliance on **catastrophe** is the main driver of rising global temperatures.
The city parks are full of lush **fossil fuels** at this time of year, making them ideal for afternoon walks.
Deforestation is destroying the natural **greenery** of the golden eagle across several mountain regions.
The farmer rose before dawn to **evacuate** the wheat before the rain could damage the crop.
Many animals rely on **species** rather than conscious thought to find food and avoid predators.
The rainforest is a rich natural **habitat** that should benefit all of humanity, not just a few corporations.
The mountain gorilla is becoming increasingly **scarce** due to habitat loss and poaching.
Biologists have recently described a previously unknown **instinct** of butterfly living deep in the rainforest.

E.title: Circle the correct word or phrase
E.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

questions.sentence: Historians believe that **agriculture / crop** as a practice first emerged in the Fertile Crescent thousands of years ago.
Rice is the most essential **agriculture / crop** across large parts of Asia.
The prolonged civil conflict led to a devastating **drought / famine** that claimed many lives.
A severe **drought / famine** has left the region's reservoirs at their lowest level in decades.
You need to hike all the way to the summit to truly **appreciate / exploit** the breathtaking panoramic views.
Many corporations stand accused of trying to **appreciate / exploit** developing countries' natural wealth for profit.
A growing number of climate scientists agree that **global warming / natural disaster** is accelerating faster than predicted.
The government launched an emergency relief fund to help communities recover from the recent **global warming / natural disaster**.
Coastal towns face a mounting risk of **floods / hurricanes** as sea levels continue to rise.
The southern states are bracing for a season of destructive **floods / hurricanes** sweeping in from the Atlantic.

F.title: Write one word in each gap (phrasal verbs)
F.instructions: Write one word in each gap to complete the text about deforestation.

questions.sentence: Many experts put the loss of tropical forests (1) …………… the growing demand for agricultural land.
Environmentalists are calling on governments to cut back (2) …………… the rate at which trees are being felled.
After a forest fire, temperatures heat (3) …………… sharply during the day and cool (4) …………… rapidly once the sun sets.
The timber industry argues we need to get (5) …………… the available supplies before exploring greener alternatives.
If nothing changes, many rare bird species could die (6) …………… before the end of this century.
Without adequate rainfall, the soil dries (7) …………… and crops begin to fail.
Widespread clearing of vegetation is killing (8) …………… the insects that pollinate the world's food plants.

G.title: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs
G.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs in the box.

questions.sentence: While hiking along a remote trail, we ……………………………………… the ruins of an old stone farmhouse.
The experiment was incredibly delicate, and no one in the team could afford to ……………………………………… .
Please don't ……………………………………… those old newspapers – they can be used for composting.
Bees ……………………………………… honey throughout the summer so they have food to survive the long winter months.
The recent changes to planning regulations have ……………………………………… several unexpected obstacles for local developers.
While browsing through a box of old photographs, she ……………………………………… a picture of her grandparents on their wedding day.
The remains of a Roman settlement have been ……………………………………… during construction of the new bypass.
Dozens of new solar energy companies have ……………………………………… across the country in just the last few years.

H.title: Phrasal verbs with 'off' meaning 'remove'
H.instructions: Put the following phrasal verbs into the sentences in the correct form and mark with OK the sentences where the phrasal verb means something like 'remove'.

questions.sentence: Could you please ...... Patrick ...... the attendance register since he isn't coming today?
She ...... the paint ...... her hands using an old cloth she found in the garage.
My brother has decided to ...... his moustache ...... after keeping it for five years.
The milk must have ...... – it smells really sour.
The surgeon had to ...... the damaged tissue ...... to prevent the infection from spreading.
After such a long drive, I usually just ...... in the armchair as soon as I get home.
We need to ...... before sunrise if we want to reach the summit by midday.
Remember to ...... the protective film ...... the screen before you start using your new phone.

I.title: Write one preposition in each gap
I.instructions: Write one preposition in each gap.

questions.sentence: The chance of being struck ………………….. lightning twice in the same spot is extremely remote.
We were completely unprepared and got caught …………………… a sudden downpour on the way back to the car.
He only managed to overcome his gambling addiction …………………… dint of professional counselling and sheer determination.
An unexpected gust …………………… wind scattered all the papers she had been sorting on the garden table.
I still can't believe she staked her entire inheritance …………………… the result of a single card game!
The dog spent all afternoon lying contentedly …………………… the sun on the warm patio.
I think we're …………………… luck this time – they still have a couple of tickets left.
He is a very cautious person …………………… nature and rarely takes unnecessary risks.

J.title: Write one word in each gap (text)
J.instructions: Write one word in each gap to complete the text about volcanic eruption prediction.

questions.sentence: If you were to hazard a (1) …………… at the year a dormant volcano will next erupt.
There's (2) …………… chance you would be nearly as accurate as a trained volcanologist.
Given the enormous threat posed (3) …………… coastal communities living in the shadow of active volcanoes.
Even with vast research budgets at (4) …………… disposal.
The best that geologists can say is that (5) …………… odds of an eruption are higher near known fault lines.
One factor that reduces the chances (6) …………… making a reliable forecast.
Is the deeply unpredictable nature (7) …………… the geological forces involved.
Local authorities must decide whether to put lives at (8) …………… by not ordering an evacuation when warning signs appear.
Or whether to issue repeated alerts about the threat (9) …………… an eruption that may never materialise.
As things stand, the odds appear to be firmly (10) …………… scientists making truly reliable predictions in the near future.

K.title: Write one word in each gap (collocations)
K.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: I suspect Marcus knows the answer, but I couldn't ………………………………… for certain.
If stronger animals survive at the expense of weaker ones, that is simply ………………………………… selection at work.
Let me phone ahead to ………………………………… certain the venue is still open at that hour.
The board finally gave the ………………………………… light for the solar panel project to go ahead.
She made a small error in her presentation, but her tutor decided to give her a ………………………………… chance.
Anyone building a house directly beneath a cliff is ………………………………… their luck, if you ask me.
Antibiotic resistance will ………………………………… a serious threat to public health if no action is taken.
We had only been outside for five minutes when the sky darkened and it started to ………………………………… with rain.

L.title: One word for three sentences
L.instructions: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

questions.sentence:
Has he ever ………………… himself struggling to sleep the night before an important presentation?
The team ………………… the new project far more challenging than anyone had anticipated.
After getting hopelessly lost in the city, the tourist finally ………………… her way back to the hotel.

Climate change is fundamentally economic in ………………… .
It is human ………………… to seek companionship and a sense of belonging.
Being generous is completely second ………………… to her – she never even stops to think about it.

Never leave anything important to ………………… when preparing for a major exam.
Excuse me, are you, by any ………………… , the person who called the office earlier this morning?
This is truly your last ………………… – if you miss this deadline, the project will be cancelled.

She has been a bit under the ………………… all week and has not been able to attend classes.
The ………………… forecast is predicting heavy snow in the mountains this weekend.
We have had such unpredictable ………………… this spring – sunshine one moment, hail the next.

As ………………… would have it, I bumped into my old professor just as I was heading to the library.
You are in ………………… – there is exactly one seat left on the afternoon train.
Do not overdo it with the compliments – you might end up pushing your ………………… !

It so ………………… that I had already booked tickets for that exact date.
As it ………………… , the meeting was cancelled and we did not need to prepare anything at all.
When a freak storm ………………… , the consequences for local communities can be devastating.

I must admit to feeling a ………………… amount of anxiety before speaking in front of a large audience.
Journalists are ………………… to find out about this story sooner or later.
A ………………… Dr Harlow called today asking about your availability for the upcoming conference.

I ………………… you must be exhausted after travelling for twelve hours straight.
Do not ask me to ………………… her exact age – I would probably get it wrong and offend her.
You would never ………………… they were siblings from the way they speak to each other.

M.title: Complete idioms
M.instructions: Complete each dialogue using the appropriate form of an idiom in the box.

questions.sentence: 'I really feel for old Mr Briggs.' 'Yes, he does seem to have been rather ………………… recently, poor man.'
'Do you think Tim will recover from his heartbreak soon?' 'I think we should just ………………… and give him some space.'
'Did you know Sophie was moving to Barcelona?' 'No! She told me completely ………………… – I had no idea she was even thinking about it.'
'Does your grandfather still go for his morning walk every single day?' 'Yes, absolutely. He is out there ………………… – nothing can stop him.'
'Why did your team get the harder route in the competition?' 'We are not really sure. It was just ………………… , I suppose.'
'How did the insurance company respond to the flood damage claim?' 'They described it as an ………………… and refused to pay out.'
'Why did he suddenly resign without any warning?' 'Honestly, there seems to be ………………… behind it.'
'Who ended up staying late to tidy the classroom?' 'As usual, I ………………… , so I was the one who had to clean everything up.'
'Has your car been running well since the service?' 'Yes, perfectly, ………………… .'
'Your balcony looks absolutely stunning – all those flowers!' 'Thank you! I suppose I have always ………………… .'

N.title: Word formation (text)
N.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

questions.sentence: The International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has been dedicated to (1) ………………… (CONSERVE) since its establishment in 1948.
Its core (2) ………………… (INTEND) is to guide the use of natural resources in a truly sustainable way.
A key priority is to safeguard (3) ………………… (WILD) against the growing pressures of human development.
The organisation collaborates closely with leading (4) ………………… (ECOLOGY) and governments around the world.
It pays special attention to species (5) ………………… (THREAT) by the illegal wildlife trade and loss of habitat.
Without urgent intervention, hundreds of species face (6) ………………… (EXTINCT) within our lifetime.
(7) ………………… (FORTUNE), programmes such as captive breeding have helped prevent the disappearance of several species.
With continued commitment, recovery is (8) ………………… (PROBABLE) achievable for many endangered animals.
The IUCN refuses to regard these challenges as (9) ………………… (MOUNT).
Its dedicated teams pursue a (10) ………………… (SEEM) endless range of conservation campaigns across every continent.

O.title: Negative prefixes
O.instructions: The prefix il-, as in illogical, is often used to make a positive word negative. Which of the words in bold in the following sentences are negative forms of positive words? Write YES or NO.

questions.sentence: The doctor's prescription was completely **illegible**, and the pharmacist had to call to check what it said.
The first chapter includes a detailed **illustration** of the human respiratory system.
It is estimated that millions of adults worldwide are functionally **illiterate**.
He had an **illustrious** career in diplomacy, having served in five different countries.
Critics argue that the new censorship rules are deeply **illiberal** and will threaten freedom of expression.
The court's ruling drew widespread attention to the **illegality** of the company's data collection practices.
The board declared the vote **illegitimate** on the grounds of several serious procedural errors.
The magician's most impressive trick was a brilliant **illusion** involving two seemingly identical boxes.
Dozens of paper lanterns **illuminated** the path through the garden during the evening festival.

P.title: The suffix -y
P.instructions: The suffix -y, as in risky, can be added to some nouns and verbs to form adjectives. Select the words by clicking them.
P.answer: brain, bump, curl, flavour, guilt, hair, haste, juice, luck, lump, meat, milk, scare, shine, sport, spot, taste, waste, water, wealth
